issue.body:
## Summary

Expands the experimental hidden `--use-agent-commander` path so hive-mind can execute every currently supported tool through `agent-commander@^0.6.0`: `claude`, `codex`, `opencode`, `agent`, `qwen`, and `gemini`.

Default behavior stays unchanged when the flag is absent: existing embedded adapters continue to handle direct tool execution. Gemini is exposed through the opt-in agent-commander path, and the Telegram `/gemini` alias adds `--tool gemini --use-agent-commander` automatically.

## What Changed

- Upgraded `agent-commander` to `^0.6.0` and validated all six tools against its exported support list.
- Extended the agent-commander adapter with model alias normalization, Claude/Codex option parity where upstream supports passthrough, Qwen/Gemini prompt support, Playwright MCP hint checks, and metadata-based result normalization.
- Added Gemini model aliases/defaults and display/model-info coverage alongside existing Claude, Codex, OpenCode, Agent, and Qwen model handling.
- Updated solve/restart validation so `--use-agent-commander` owns the agent-commander execution path while direct execution remains the default path.
- Added Telegram `/gemini` alias support, Gemini queue tracking, documentation updates, changeset text, and the issue 1043 case-study evidence.

## Upstream Follow-Up

`agent-commander@0.6.0` now supports all six tool names, but Qwen and Gemini still lack some parity surfaces available to Claude/Codex/OpenCode/Agent, especially prompt-file handling and executable/env/extra-arg passthrough. Reported upstream:

- https://github.com/link-assistant/agent-commander/issues/35

Earlier parity issues are now closed:

- https://github.com/link-assistant/agent-commander/issues/29
- https://github.com/link-assistant/agent-commander/issues/30

## Verification

Post-merge with current `origin/main` (`1.62.1`):

- `node tests/test-agent-commander-option.mjs`
- `node tests/test-issue-882-fixes.mjs`
- `node tests/model-info.test.mjs`
- `node tests/test-telegram-bot-command-aliases.mjs`
- `node tests/solve-queue-tool-tracking.test.mjs`
- `node tests/solve-queue.test.mjs`
- `node tests/test-qwen-support.mjs`
- `npm run format:check`
- `npm run lint`
- `npm test` (all 81 selected test files passed)

No UI changes, so screenshots are not applicable.

Resolves #1043

issue.body:
During hive-mind PR https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/pull/1044 for issue https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/issues/1043, I reviewed the latest JavaScript package `agent-commander@0.4.3` against hive-mind's embedded tool adapters.

The current hive-mind integration can route basic execution through `agent-commander`, but several hive-mind options still cannot be expressed through the public agent-commander API without falling back to direct tool-specific command construction.

Needed parity surface:

- Claude: thinking budget / effort env handling, 1M context disable behavior, sub-session auto-compact envs, MCP config without Playwright, useless/disallowed tool flags, read-only / permission modes, verbose/fallback/resume/session behavior, and custom binary/env passthrough.
- Codex: reasoning effort mapping, context window / auto-compact config, MCP config generation, sandbox/approval mode parity, custom binary/env passthrough, and the prompt-file path used for large prompts.
- OpenCode: config/permission env parity, custom binary/env passthrough, and prompt-file or equivalent large-prompt support.
- `agent`: hive-mind verbose handling, resume/no-fork equivalents where supported, permission/config parity, custom binary/env passthrough, and full JSON usage/result reporting.

Request:

Provide typed agent-commander options or a documented raw args/env/config passthrough that lets hive-mind control these features only through agent-commander when `--use-agent-commander` is enabled. This would let hive-mind remove remaining adapter-level compromises while keeping default embedded execution unchanged.

repository.name: agent-commander

issue.title: Expose hive-mind execution parity options for claude/codex/opencode/agent

issue.body:
During hive-mind PR https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/pull/1044 for issue https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/issues/1043, the experimental `--use-agent-commander` adapter had to best-effort parse raw/plain output from `agent-commander@0.4.3` to populate hive-mind's solve result fields.

Hive-mind currently consumes normalized metadata from its embedded adapters, including:

- success / non-zero exit classification
- session id
- usage-limit detection and reset time/timezone
- human-readable result summary
- Anthropic total cost where available
- public pricing estimate where available
- detailed model usage
- stream token usage
- sub-agent call summaries
- execution error classification

The current agent-commander result object has useful `exitCode`, `output.plain`, `output.parsed`, `sessionId`, and `usage` fields, but hive-mind still needs adapter-specific parsing to derive the complete normalized result shape.

Request:

Expose a stable normalized result metadata object from agent-commander for each supported tool (`claude`, `codex`, `opencode`, `agent`) so callers like hive-mind can avoid parsing tool-specific output while still reporting costs, usage limits, summaries, and failures consistently.

repository.description: A JavaScript library to control agents enclosed in CLI commands like Anthropic Claude Code CLI
repository.name: agent-commander

issue.title: Expose normalized result metadata for hive-mind solve sessions

issue.body:
While integrating `agent-commander@0.6.0` into `link-assistant/hive-mind` for issue https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/issues/1043, basic `qwen` and `gemini` tool launch support is present, but two parity gaps remain for hive-mind's generated solve sessions.

Gaps found:

1. Prompt-file/large-prompt handling is still limited to `claude`, `codex`, `opencode`, and `agent`.
   - `src/index.mjs` has `PROMPT_FILE_TOOLS = new Set(['claude', 'codex', 'opencode', 'agent'])`.
   - `qwen` and `gemini` currently pass the combined prompt inline through CLI args (`-p`), which is risky for hive-mind's large issue/PR prompts.

2. Raw passthrough parity is missing for `qwen` and `gemini`.
   - `claude`, `codex`, `opencode`, and `agent` builders accept `executable`, `extraEnv`, and `extraArgs`.
   - `qwen` and `gemini` builders hardcode `qwen`/`gemini` and ignore those passthrough options.
   - The README also documents `--tool-executable` as applying only to `claude`, `codex`, `opencode`, or `agent`.

Expected behavior:

- `qwen` and `gemini` should either support `promptFile`/temporary prompt files and raw `executable`/`extraEnv`/`extraArgs` passthrough like the other tools, or expose/document these as intentionally unsupported so callers can branch on capability metadata.

Why this matters:

- Hive-mind solve prompts are often large and contain issue details, PR feedback, repository context, and workflow instructions.
- Hive-mind also supports per-tool executable overrides for embedded adapters; the agent-commander path can preserve that for the first four tools but not yet for `qwen`/`gemini`.

Observed with:

- `agent-commander@0.6.0`
- Hive-mind PR: https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/pull/1044
